Members Open Meeting: Thursday 9th April St Ives Corn Exchange 1.20 for 2.00pm Articles and features for the May newsletter should be sent to Anne by 17th April: editor@u3aivo.uk Newsletter April 2026 Website: u3aivo.uk, email: info@u3aivo.uk Or call 07458 197372 All Members Open Meetings are held at the Corn Exchange, St Ives 1.20pm for 2.00pm start. Please note: We kindly ask that members wishing to discuss membership matters or to book outings or theatre trips please arrive early to avoid disruption. Committee members and the Group Support Team will be available after the meeting to answer questions and discuss any matters of concern. In the Wake of Ernest Shackleton Speaker: Dave Pinion Dave Pinion is a retired BT engineer who worked on International Optical Fibre Submarine Cable Systems. He ﬁrst went to Antarctica in 2023 and fell in love with it – he caught the “Polar Bug”. Dave will take you on a journey from South Georgia, past the South Sandwich Islands and deep into the Weddell Sea where the Endurance became trapped in the ice in 1915. Visit Elephant Island where 22 men lived for 4 months whilst Shackleton sailed 800 miles to get help, meeting penguins, seal pups, humpback whales on the way.
Outings 2 Thursday 16th July 2026 Two venues in one day Barnsdale Gardens (Rutland) and Stamford Arrive 10am for a morning visit to Barnsdale Gardens Rutland. Time to explore the large collection of individual gardens created by Geoff Hamilton for BBC Gardeners' World. Leave Barnsdale 1pm for short journey to Stamford. Afternoon free time in Stamford . A premier English market town renowned as one of the ﬁnest "stone towns" in the country, boasting over 600 listed limestone buildings, and stunning Georgian architecture. Explore the historic high street, independent shops, and the scenic River Welland. Refreshments available for purchase at both venues. Cost £32, includes driver’s gratuity Outings Gressenhall Farm and Workhouse Thursday 16th April WAITING LIST ONLY British Motor Museum Gaydon, Warwickshire Tuesday 19th May—See next page Charlecote House and Upton House, Tuesday 30 th June WAITING LIST ONLY Theatre Trips ABBA Voyage The ABBA Arena, Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park Sunday 3rd May WAITING LIST ONLY Operation Mincemeat Milton Keynes Theatre Wednesday 10th June WAITING LIST ONLY
The British Motor Museum Tuesday 19th May This Outing will be a thrilling day out full of motoring history with hands on experience of many of the museum’s wonderful cars and exhibits. A trip well worth considering with appeal for all members men and ladies alike. The British Motor Museum in Gaydon Warwickshire houses the world’s largest collection of historic British cars. More than 300 vehicles on display, drawn from the collections of the British Motor Heritage Trust and the Jaguar Heritage Trust. In 1968 the formation of the British Leyland Motor Corporation brought together several motor manufacturers and marques, including Austin, Jaguar, Morris, MG, Riley, Rover, Standard Triumph and Wolseley. As many of these companies already maintained their own historic vehicle collections a centralised Leyland collection was established in 1975 to manage these. Then in December 2014 the museum’s collection was recognised by Arts Council England as a Designated Collection, placing it among the country’s exceptional cultural collections. As a child growing up in Birmingham the home town of British Leyland I have a particular soft spot for this museum and remember visiting with my car mad family. The museum is a far cry from those days very swish now. We still have some seats available for this trip if you would like to sign up via the Outings blue envelope on the website or at the Outings desk at the next meeting. See you then Gill Jones https:// stivescambs.u3asite.uk/ u3a_events/british-motor- museum/ 3 British Motor Museum
4 Events BOOK HERE
This is a 2-hour 'pop-up' workshop for fully and partially retired members of St Ives u3a who are interested in learning about how a knowledge of existentialism can help them to enhance their lives in retirement. Participants The workshop is suitable for participants with little or no prior knowledge or understanding of existentialism, but there is an assumption that those present will have a curiosity about existential ideas and a willingness to participate in discussions and activities. Content There will be a brief review of some of the most relevant existential ideas of the key modern-day thinkers from the mid-1800s to the present day. We will discuss existentialism's core ideas and consider how we might apply these ideas in our own lives. We will also debate such questions as: - Can retirement act as a catalyst for re-evaluating life's purpose? - How can we live life more meaningfully following the end of our working lives? - Can uncertainty and change be liberating? Depending on time constraints, we will undertake one or two activities to help those who wish to make changes, moving forward. Further information 1. A reading list will be provided for those participants wishing to study the subject further in their own time. 2. Some suggestions for home- based activities will be provided for those wishing to apply the existential ideas to their own lives. 3. Should sufﬁcient interest in existentialism be generated by the workshop, it is likely that a monthly u3a group would be established in the autumn of 2026 (date, time and venue to be decided). To book go to the Events page: https://stivescambs.u3asite.uk/ u3a_groups/events/ Existentialism and its Applications in Retirement The Golden Lion Hotel Thursday 4th June 2-4pm 5 Authenticity
Theatre Trips 6 Booking Procedure for Outings, Theatre Trips and Events PLEASE DO NOT PAY FOR TRIPS BEFORE YOUR BOOKING HAS BEEN CONFIRMED • Bookings can be made at Open Meetings or on the website • Theatre Trips: https://stivescambs.u3asite.uk/u3a_groups/theatre - trips/ • Outings: https://stivescambs.u3asite.uk/u3a_groups/outings/ • Events: https://stivescambs.u3asite.uk/u3a_groups/events/ • Outings: Sue Belcher (07778425052 — Strictly for members with no internet) • When booking a trip on the web page, click on the blue envelope, please DO NOT click on 'CONTACT' at the very top of the page. • ALWAYS BOOK BEFORE PAYING. All bookings will be confirmed. • BACS payment preferred please, either online or by going into your branch • Payment: St Ives (Cambs) U3A Social Account • Sort code: 40 - 16 - 08 Account: 74557352 • Ref: u3a membership number and trip name • If no alternative, cheques made payable to: St Ives (Cambs) U3A Social Account • Payment to be made at least six weeks prior to the trip Twelfth Night TOLETHORPE HALL, nr STAMFORD Stamford Shakespeare Company Saturday 25th July 2.30pm Shakespeare’s fast-paced comedy with love triangles, mistaken identities and practical jokes arrives in a romantic Tudor garden where the interwoven action turns to absurdity, silliness and downright fun. 600-seater permanently covered raked auditorium. Snacks and drink available or bring your own picnic. £25, pay by 23rd April, travel cost TBA
Group News 7 Strollers Group When we set up the Strollers Group during last summer’s Open Day there was clearly lots of enthusiasm for folks to get go outdoors, enjoy some short walks and social time but it was a shaky start! We met up for a couple of very short strolls with me encouraging the group to come equipped with walking poles to keep everyone on two feet. Roger and Sophie Brown immediately proved to be very good leaders with an understanding of how far the group could manage walking and tailored regular routes to suit. Then only six months later the progress that the group has made in terms of conﬁdence and ability is quite breath taking. I turned up a few weeks ago expecting to go for a very short puddly plod but instead Roger was checking in 15 walkers and all but two new ones came equipped with walking poles. We set off for an explore around the lanes of Houghton and I was amazed at what a vibrant conﬁdent bunch of walkers they have become. We walked at a fair pace with the lovely hum of social conversation between friends. Then into the cafe for coffee and I was blown away when Sophie Brown announced that she is aiming to take part in a 13 mile walk in the summer and is in training to do this! This so far exceeds my wildest dreams for the group. Roger told me that they have not missed a week through the winter and they walk every fortnight. The conversation was incredible and I am totally in awe of the u3a community. Sophie had previously cycled the length of Jordan in her youth! I took some photos along the way and Roger is now gearing up to post on the website. What a morning! Gill Jones Group Support Team The team encourages new groups to get off the ground and offers support to Group Leaders when they need it. As our membership grows we need more groups and the team would love one or two of you to join us to make sure we can offer as much help as possible. If you can spare a couple of hours a month and attend a short monthly meeting, then this might be for you. To ﬁnd out more please email: gst@u3aivo.uk
8 Membership Renewals Your membership of St Ives u3a is due for renewal on 1st April 2026. Payments are welcome before this date. The annual fee remains unchanged at £13. Associate members. If you belong to another u3a and pay a full fee to them (excluding Cambridge u3a) the membership fee payable to St Ives u3a is reduced to £9.50. Preferred method of payment Our preferred method of payment is by Standing Order. If you have a current standing order in place for payment to be made to St Ives u3a on 1st April each year, you need do nothing. If you are uncertain about having set one up, please check before paying by an alternative method. Alternative Payment Methods If you have not already arranged a standing order payment, the options are: BACS payment (online) to: St Ives Cambs u3a account: Sort code 40-40-35, Account number 41529994 For the reference please use your Surname, followed by ﬁrst name, followed by your membership number (shown on your membership card) e.g. SMITH, Fred. 6789. Cheque: made payable to: St Ives (Cambs) u3a, for £13 (£9.50 if full fee paid to another u3a) - print your full name and membership number on the back - either - post it to the Membership Secretary 15 Heath Road Warboys Huntingdon PE28 2TQ or bring it to the May Open Meeting. Cash: payable at the May Open Meeting. Membership cards Your existing card remains valid just as long as you renew your membership! The Beacon system will alert us to any members who do not renew. And ﬁnally - If you do not intend to renew, please let me know using the blue envelope email link on the Contact Us page of the website. Ian Brennand Membership Secretary https://stivescambs.u3asite.uk/contact/
